free_text: Target-fragment angular distributions for the
interaction of 86 MeV/A C-12 with Au-197.
Two thin Au targets consisted of an evaporated deposit
of metallic Au on a 13.4-mg/cm**2 Al-backing.  The
'forward' and 'backward' Au deposits had thicknesses
of 201.8 and 258.2-mug/cm**2 in the first experiment
and thicknesses of 79.6 and 101.4-mug/cm**2 in second
experiment.
Fragments emerging from the target were caught in
17.6-mg/cm**2 mylar or approx.35-mg/cm**2 carbon foil.
In the second experiment, fragments emerging at angles
of 0 to 27 degree were caught in a 'hyper-pure' carbon
foil of thickness 33-mg/cm**2.
The results of the first and second experiments agreed
in general within experimental uncertainty, generally
+-10% and were combined to give the final results.
*By authors*.The catcher foils were divided into ten
pieces corresponding to the angular intervals of
degree. These pieces were assayed by
gamma-spectroscopy beginning a few minutes after the
end of irradiation and continuing for period of up
to 3 months in CERN, LBL and Oregon State university.
.Firestreak model. Hydrodynamical model.
The laboratory-frame angular distributions were
transformed into the moving frame using various
assumptions about the moving frame velocity.
CERN SC synchrocyclotron
The biggest potential systematic difficulty with the
measurements concerns the effect of target thickness
and configuration upon the experimental results,
principally for the heavy fragments it is
-stopping of the target fragments in the Au deposit,
-large-angle scattering of target fragments from the
Al target backing or the Au target material,
-small-angle scattering of target fragments in the
Au target material.
No information about source of uncertainties
Uncertainty in monitor reaction
S B
Last checking has been done.
SD: Syntax in MONIT-REF corrected. Author's
name corrected.  Corrections in Subent  002.

(zeros to right side of the value were added).
